title: ASML Earnings Q4 2025 | Rekord-Aufträge, KI-Boom und der Wachstumspfad 2026
description: In dieser Episode analysieren wir die starken Finanzergebnisse von ASML, das das Jahr 2025 mit einem Umsatz von 32,7 Milliarden Euro und einem massiven Auftragseingang von 13,2 Milliarden Euro im vierten Quartal abschloss,. Wir beleuchten den Einfluss des KI-Booms auf die steigende Nachfrage nach EUV-Technologie sowie die ersten Umsatzrealisierungen bei den neuartigen High-NA-Systemen und wie der optimistische Ausblick für 2026 mit Umsatzerwartungen von bis zu 39 Milliarden Euro und ein neues Ak

title: ASML Soars, Texas Instruments Jumps, AT&T Higher After Earnings Beats Expectations.
description: On this episode of Stock Movers:- ASML (ASML NA) shares soar after orders in the fourth quarter far exceeded analysts’ expectations and the Dutch semiconductor equipment maker announced job cuts to boost efficiency. - Texas Instruments (TXN) shares jump after giving a surprisingly robust forecast for the first quarter, indicating that demand for industrial equipment and vehicles is recovering from a rough patch. - AT&T (T) shares rise after reporting fourth-quarter profit and revenue that beat a

title: Inside ASML’s Earnings Beat, Netflix’s Podcasting Move & the Largest Data Center Acquisition Ever
description:
Ed Elson is joined by Andrew Gardiner, Head of European Technology Equity Research at Citi, to discuss takeaways from ASML’s earnings. Then Ed and Scott Galloway discuss Netflix’s move to offer podcasts. Finally, Ed dives into a $40 billion deal to buy one of the world’s biggest data center operators.

title: ASML Earnings Signal AI Demand Holding Strong
description:
Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde breaks down ASML’s earnings as the AI boom continues to fuel demand for its cutting-edge chip-making machines. Plus, deals to develop data centers just keep coming, among them Microsoft and UK startup NScale inking a fourth deal. And Apple updates its top-of-the-line iPad Pro and other products with its own in-house chip.

title: ASML’s Earnings & How to Follow Earnings Season
description:

Travis Hoium, Lou Whiteman, and Rachel Warren discuss:

- ASML earnings

- The Fed moving markets

- How the Fed is stuck between a slowing economy and inflation

title: ASML Holding (ASML), 2025 Q2 earnings digest
description: $ASML has released its Q2 earnings, highlighting a solid EPS of $6.86, exceeding expectations, and total revenue of $8.95 billion, up 31.07% year-over-year. Key risks include macroeconomic uncertainty impacting customer investments and continued tariff discussions. In terms of profitability, gross margins reached 53.7%. Future outlook remains optimistic, with strong demand in AI driving growth. However, customers are cautious regarding CAPEX based on geopolitical tensions. For more details, chec

title: ASML Q2 Earnings 2025
description: ASML's Q2 2025 results stunned the market with record revenues and a major backlog, but future guidance carries a note of caution amid geopolitical headwinds. Ray and Mark break down the numbers, explore the tech demand driving ASML, and highlight what risks lie ahead for investors.

title: ASML (ASML), 2025 Q1 earnings digest
description: $ASML has released its Q1 2025 earnings. Key stats: EPS of $6.83 (+4.40% YoY) surpassed expectations; revenue of $8.82B (+56.24% YoY) also beat forecasts. Strong growth from EUV and logic systems is a highlight, but risks from macroeconomic uncertainties and tariffs loom.

title: Europe’s irreplaceable innovator: ASML’s CEO on powering the digital future
description:
Christophe Fouquet, CEO of ASML, tells Jean-François Manzoni how the world’s only EUV lithography machine maker became Europe’s most indispensable company – and what comes next.
Few companies can claim to sit at the center of modern technological progress. ASML is one of them. The Dutch multinational is the only company in the world capable of producing extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography machines – the technology behind the smallest, fastest, and most powerful semiconductor chips.
Without ASM

title: 58. "Bankenstress en wij krijgen hoogtevrees van ASML en TSMC"
description:
In de VS gaan de koersen van banken behoorlijk onderuit en ook in Europa is de markt nerveus. Zit er meer ellende aan te komen?
Als u denkt van wel, dan raden wij u onze gloednieuwe optiecursus aan. Professioneel optiehandelaar Sven Hubens legt in deze cursus uitgebreid uit hoe u opties gebruikt om risico's in uw portfolio te verlagen en de risico/rendementsverhouding te verbeteren.

title: The Truth About Buying Stocks at High Valuations
description:
Is it better to buy the bubble or miss the boom? Join Neil Woodford and Jon Adair as they discuss the week’s major market developments. In this episode, they discuss the implications of high valuations on future returns, the performance of Neil's Top 40 investment strategy, and the significance of accurate productivity data in the UK. 

The conversation also touches on the ongoing trade tensions with China and the role of central banks in shaping economic growth and important announcements from

title: 56. ASML, Besi & helaas weer box 3
description:
We beginnen in Het BeursCafé met het zuur, want uit de doorberekeningen van het CPB blijkt dat de meeste politieke partijen de belasting op vermogen verder willen verhogen. 
Maar na het zuur komt het zoet, want de beurzen staan op een all time high, professioneel optiehandelaar Sven Hubens legt uit hoe u de risk/reward van uw portfolio met opties kan verbeteren en Jelle Postma, co-founder van Capler, praat u helemaal bij over de private equity markt.

title: ASML's Advanced Packaging Play: What Investors Need to Know
description:
ASML is known for one thing: the most advanced EUV lithography machines on the planet. But there's a quieter story developing in their advanced packaging division that's worth paying attention to.
While the Fab Five have seen a recent sell-off, the second half of 2026 is shaping up to be a revenue inflection point for the semiconductor equipment sector.
